payload.question: When the funding pack finally lands on a desk, whose desk is it — an angel writing a $25k check, an accelerator screener with 90 seconds, or an institutional associate building a memo?
payload.why_it_matters: These three readers reject for different reasons; a pack tuned for the wrong one fails silently.
payload.default_if_unanswered: Pre-seed angel and accelerator screener; institutional-grade rigor, screener-grade brevity.

payload.question: Version one must prove itself on one real, currently-stalled build. Which one is the subject of the first contract?
payload.why_it_matters: A tool validated only on demos is a demo; the first contract decides what "works" means.
payload.default_if_unanswered: Signet itself — the engine builds under its own contract (genesis dogfood).

payload.question: In your own API dollars, what is the hard cap per build phase before the engine must stop and put the decision back in your hands?
payload.why_it_matters: Silent overspend is the exact waste this product exists to kill; an unset cap is an unlimited one.
payload.default_if_unanswered: $25 per phase, hard stop, resumable only by an explicit founder decision.

payload.question: If the builder is certain you would love a feature the contract does not name, may it build it anyway?
payload.why_it_matters: This single yes/no decides whether scope drift is a judgment call or a rejected event; judgment calls are how codebases bloat.
payload.default_if_unanswered: No. Uncontracted work is rejected and recorded, however good the idea; good ideas go into the next contract.

payload.question: Name one object — not software — whose feel the founder-facing surface should be mistaken for.
payload.why_it_matters: "Not generic or cheap" is unenforceable; a concrete reference object is a testable taste anchor.
payload.default_if_unanswered: A finely typeset legal instrument on heavy paper, executed with a wax seal.

payload.question: Which decisions must never be made without you, even if waiting costs days?
payload.why_it_matters: Everything not on this list the engine will decide alone; an incomplete list surrenders rights you meant to keep.
payload.default_if_unanswered: Locking the contract, judging every human gate, raising any budget cap, and the kill/iterate call.

payload.question: You believe contract discipline — not smarter models — is what cuts the waste. If the first governed build fails to beat your current workflow on rework and spend, is that a kill or an iterate?
payload.why_it_matters: Deciding the failure rule before the result exists is the only version of the decision that is honest.
payload.default_if_unanswered: Iterate once with a revised contract; a second miss is a kill.

payload.answer: Signet itself. The engine ships under its own locked contract — genesis dogfood — then the next contract is one of my stalled artificialBRIDGE apps.
No. Drift rejected, always. Good ideas go in the next contract.

payload.title: Signet v1 — the open-source build contract engine
Signet v1 — the open-source build contract engine

payload.prompt: Read examples/signet-genesis/interrogation — would a non-technical founder understand every word, and does every question earn its place?
Open the Signet UI. Is it something you would be proud to put in front of an investor?

payload.intent: add a template marketplace (great idea, not in contract)
